chapter: Chapter: When People Rebel – 1857 and After

questions.question: In which year did the Revolt of 1857 take place?
questions.answer: The Revolt of 1857 took place in the year 1857.

questions.question: Where did the Revolt of 1857 begin?
questions.answer: The revolt began in Meerut.

questions.question: Who were the Indian soldiers in the British army called?
questions.answer: Indian soldiers in the British army were called sepoys.

questions.question: Who was the last Mughal emperor during the Revolt of 1857?
questions.answer: Bahadur Shah Zafar was the last Mughal emperor.

questions.question: Who was declared the leader of the rebels in Delhi?
questions.answer: Bahadur Shah Zafar was declared the leader of the rebels.

questions.question: Who led the revolt in Jhansi?
questions.answer: Rani Lakshmibai led the revolt in Jhansi.

questions.question: Who led the revolt in Kanpur?
questions.answer: Nana Sahib led the revolt in Kanpur.

questions.question: Who led the revolt in Bihar?
questions.answer: Kunwar Singh led the revolt in Bihar.

questions.question: Who led the revolt in Lucknow?
questions.answer: Begum Hazrat Mahal led the revolt in Lucknow.

questions.question: Which policy allowed the British to annex Indian states without heirs?
questions.answer: The Doctrine of Lapse allowed the British to annex Indian states.

questions.question: What was one cause of the Revolt of 1857?
questions.answer: One cause was the use of greased cartridges in rifles.

questions.question: Why were the greased cartridges controversial?
questions.answer: They were believed to be greased with cow and pig fat, offending religious beliefs.

questions.question: Who was Mangal Pandey?
questions.answer: Mangal Pandey was a sepoy who played a role in starting the revolt.

questions.question: Which city became the centre of the revolt?
questions.answer: Delhi became the centre of the revolt.

questions.question: What happened to Bahadur Shah Zafar after the revolt?
questions.answer: He was captured by the British and exiled to Rangoon.

questions.question: Which groups participated in the Revolt of 1857?
questions.answer: Sepoys, peasants, rulers and common people participated.

questions.question: What was one major reason for the failure of the revolt?
questions.answer: One reason was lack of unity among the rebels.

questions.question: What happened to the East India Company after the revolt?
questions.answer: The British government ended the rule of the East India Company.

questions.question: Who took control of India after 1857?
questions.answer: The British Crown took control of India.

questions.question: Why was the Revolt of 1857 important?
questions.answer: It was the first large-scale rebellion against British rule.

questions.question: What was the role of sepoys in the revolt?
questions.answer: Sepoys started the revolt against British officers.

questions.question: What change was made in the British army after the revolt?
questions.answer: The British increased the number of European soldiers.

questions.question: Why did many Indian rulers join the revolt?
questions.answer: They were unhappy with British annexation policies.

questions.question: What message did the Revolt of 1857 send to the British?
questions.answer: It showed strong resistance against colonial rule.

questions.question: What is another name for the Revolt of 1857?
questions.answer: It is also called the First War of Independence.
